{{Short description|National association football team}}
{{About|the men's team|the women's team|Solomon Islands women's national football team}}
{{Use dmy dates|date=May 2018}}
{{Infobox national football team
| Name               = Solomon Islands
| Badge              = Flag of Solomon Islands.svg
| Nickname           = [[Bonito]]s
| Association        = [[Solomon Islands Football Federation]] (SIFF)
| Confederation      = [[Oceania Football Confederation|OFC]] (Oceania)
| Coach              = {{flagicon|ENG}} [[Ben Cahn]] (interim)
| Captain            = [[Joses Nawo]]
| Most caps          = [[Henry Fa'arodo]] (64)
| Top scorer         = [[Commins Menapi]] (34)
| Home Stadium       = [[National Stadium (Solomon Islands)|National Stadium]],<br>[[Lawson Tama Stadium]]
| FIFA Trigramme     = SOL
| FIFA Rank          = {{FIFA World Rankings|SOL}}
| FIFA max           = 120
| FIFA max date      = October 2007, April 2008
| FIFA min           = 200
| FIFA min date      = January–March 2016
| Elo Rank           = {{World Football Elo Ratings|Solomon Islands}}
| Elo max            = 109
| Elo max date       = September 2005
| Elo min            = 177
| Elo min date       = November 2016
| pattern_la1        = _blueborder
| pattern_b1         = _slo24h
| pattern_ra1        = _blueborder
| pattern_sh1        = _shorts
| pattern_so1        =
| leftarm1           = FFF200
| body1              = FFF200
| rightarm1          = FFF200
| shorts1            = 0000FF
| socks1             = FFF200
| pattern_la2        =
| pattern_b2         = _SOL_a22
| pattern_ra2        =
| pattern_sh2        = _shorts
| pattern_so2        =
| leftarm2           = 157D65 
| body2              = 157D65 
| rightarm2          = 157D65 
| shorts2            = FFFFFF
| socks2             = FFFFFF
| First game         = {{nowrap begin}}{{fb|SOL|1956}} 6–3 {{fb-rt|New Hebrides|1963}}<br>([[Suva]], [[Fiji]]; 30 August 1963){{nowrap end}}
| Largest win        = {{nowrap begin}}{{fb|SOL}} 16–0 {{fb-rt|COK}}<br>([[Papeete]], [[Tahiti]]; 21 August 1995){{nowrap end}}
| Largest loss       = {{fb|TAH|1946}} 18–0 {{fb-rt|SOL|1956}}<br>([[Suva]], [[Fiji]]; 8 December 1963)
| Regional name      = [[OFC Men's Nations Cup|OFC Nations Cup]]
| Regional cup apps  = 8
| Regional cup first = [[1980 OFC Nations Cup|1980]]
| Regional cup best  = Runners-up ([[2004 OFC Nations Cup|2004]])
| abolished          =

|3rdRegional cup apps=11|3rdRegional name=[[MSG Prime Minister's Cup|Melanesia Cup / MSG Prime Minister's Cup]]|3rdRegional cup first=[[1988 Melanesia Cup|1988]]|3rdRegional cup best=[[Melanesia Cup]]: '''Champions'''<br>([[1994 Melanesia Cup|1994]])<br>[[MSG Prime Minister's Cup]]: '''Champions'''<br>([[2023 MSG Prime Minister's Cup|2023]])

}}

The '''Solomon Islands men's national football team''' is the national [[association football]] team of the [[Solomon Islands]], administered by the [[Solomon Islands Football Federation]]. The Solomon Islands national football team was founded in 1978. They were officially recognised by [[FIFA]] a decade later, in 1988.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.fifa.com/associations/association=sol/index.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070623100219/http://www.fifa.com/associations/association=sol/index.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=23 June 2007|title=Member Association – Solomon Islands|website=FIFA.com|language=en-GB|access-date=2018-09-05}}</ref>

==History==
During the 2004 Oceania World Cup qualification/Oceania Cup the team drew 2–2 with Australia and qualified for the second leg. In the second leg, the Solomon Islands national men's team were thrashed by Australia 5–1 and 6–0 in the two matches, with Australia qualifying for the [[2005 FIFA Confederations Cup]].

The Solomons got a second chance against the Socceroos in a two-legged series in September 2005, this time with the winner advancing to a two-legged series against [[CONMEBOL]]'s fifth-place finisher for a berth in the [[2006 FIFA World Cup]], and the team was thrashed by Australia 7–0 on the first leg and 2–1 in the second played at home.

The Solomons were knocked out of the [[2010 FIFA World Cup]] – having got off to a good start winning every game in their qualifying group and comfortably progressing to the knockout rounds, defeats to [[New Caledonia national football team|New Caledonia]] and then to [[Vanuatu national football team|Vanuatu]] saw them knocked out of the competition.

In 2012, the Solomon Islands held the [[2012 OFC Nations Cup]] which was also the second round of World Cup qualifying for the [[2014 FIFA World Cup]] where they finished in fourth place after qualifying through to the knockout stage by defeating Papua New Guinea and having draws against Fiji and New Zealand. They lost in the semi-final after they lost to the champions Tahiti after [[Jonathan Tehau]] scored the only goal. They later lost to New Zealand in the third-place playoff. The third round of World Cup qualifying saw the team finish bottom of the group after only taking one win against [[Tahiti national football team|Tahiti]].

After first taking charge of the team in 2017, Spaniard [[Felipe Vega-Arango]] was appointed for his second stint in June 2021.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.oceaniafootball.com/solomon-islands-appoint-new-coach/|title=Solomon Islands appoint new coach|date=23 June 2021|access-date=24 June 2021|archive-date=23 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210623231033/https://www.oceaniafootball.com/solomon-islands-appoint-new-coach/|url-status=live}}</ref>

In 2019, they went on a three-week training tour in [[Netherlands]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.siff.com.sb/historic-training-camp-in-netherlands-wrap-up/|title=Historic training camp in Netherlands wrap-up|date=5 June 2019 |publisher=siff.com.sb|access-date=17 July 2022|archive-date=2 July 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220702223844/https://www.siff.com.sb/historic-training-camp-in-netherlands-wrap-up/|url-status=live}}</ref>

In June 2023, Solomon Islands played their friendly match against Southeast Asia countries, [[Singapore national football team|Singapore]] and [[Malaysia national football team|Malaysia]]. Solomon Island won the [[2023 MSG Prime Minister's Cup]] after winning against [[Papua New Guinea national football team|Papua New Guinea]] (3–1), [[Vanuatu national football team|Vanuatu]] (1–0) and [[New Caledonia national football team|New Caledonia]] (1–0) as [[Raphael Lea'i]] clinched the golden boot as the tournament top scorer with four goals.

== Team image ==

===Kit sponsorship===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:left;"
|-
! Kit lier
! Period
|-
|align="Left"|{{flagicon|Italy}} [[Lotto Sport Italia|Lotto]] ||2004–2013
|-
|align="Left"|{{flagicon|New Zealand}} Bocini ||2014
|-
|align="Left"|{{flagicon|Unknown}} OFC || 2015
|-
|None || 2016
|-
|align="Left"|{{flagicon|Australia}} Veto || 2017
|-
|align="Left"|{{flagicon|China}} UCAN||2019–2022
|-
|align="Left"|{{flagicon|China}} SUNAIS||2022–2024
|-
|align="Left"|{{flagicon|Australia}} Veto|| 2024–2026
|-
|align="Left"|{{flagicon|Australia}} KPI|| 2026–
|}

=== Sponsors ===

* {{Flagicon|Australia}} [[KEEP PUSHING IMPOSSIBLE|KPI]]
* {{Flagicon|Malaysia}} [[Telekom Malaysia|Telekom]]
* {{Flagicon|Malaysia}} [[Capo Apparel]]

==Results and fixtures==
{{Main|Solomon Islands national football team results (1963–1999)|Solomon Islands national football team results (2000–present)}}

The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#CCFFCC|Win|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

===2025===
{{footballbox collapsible
| format=1
| round      = [[2025 MSG Prime Minister's Cup – Men's tournament#Group A|2025 MSG Prime Minister's Cup]]
| date       = 13 November
| time       = {{UTZ|16:00|10}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|PNG}}
| score      = 1–0
| report     = https://www.soccerway.com/match/papua-new-guinea-OjkU0PHe/solomon-islands-fF5OOS92/?mid=40TCUcRF
| team2      = {{fb|SOL}}
| goals1     = *[[Pala Paul|P. Paul]] {{goal|75}}
| goals2     =
| stadium    = [[PNG Football Stadium]]
| location   = [[Port Moresby]], Papua New Guinea
| attendance = 
| referee    = 
| result     = L
}}
{{football box collapsible
|format= 1
|round= [[2025 MSG Prime Minister's Cup – Men's tournament#Semi-finals|2025 MSG Prime Minister's Cup semi-final]]
|date= 20 November
|time= {{UTZ|19:00|10}}
|team1= {{fb-rt|VAN}}
|score= 2–1
|team2= {{fb|SOL}}
|goals1=
*[[Malakai Andrew|Andrew]] {{Goal|32}}
*[[Miller Loloa|Loloa]] {{Goal|40}}
|goals2=
*[[David Supa|Supa]] {{Goal|44}}
|stadium= [[PNG Football Stadium]]
|location= [[Port Moresby]], Papua New Guinea
|attendance=
|referee=
|report= https://www.soccerway.com/match/solomon-islands-fF5OOS92/vanuatu-ng4XIBFH/?mid=MH3SrFBU
|result= L
}}
{{football box collapsible
|format= 1
|round= [[2025 MSG Prime Minister's Cup – Men's tournament#Third place playoff|2025 MSG Prime Minister's Cup third place match]]
|date= 22 November
|time= {{UTZ|17:00|10}}
|team1= {{fb-rt|FIJ}}
|score= 1–1<br>Abandoned
|team2= {{fb|SOL}}
|goals1= *[[Christopher Wasasala|Wasasala]] {{Goal|22|pen.}}
|goals2= *[[Rovu Boyers|Boyers]] {{Goal|35|pen.}}
|stadium= [[PNG Football Stadium]]
|location= [[Port Moresby]], Papua New Guinea
|attendance=
|referee=
|report= https://news.pngfacts.com/2025/11/msg-cup-fijisolomon-islands-3rd4th.html
|result= V
}}

===2026===
{{footballbox collapsible|format=1
| round      = [[2026 FIFA Series (men's matches)|2026 FIFA Series]]
| date       = 27 March
| time       = {{UTZ|15:30|7}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|SOL}}
| score      = 2–10
| report     = https://www.espn.com/soccer/match/_/gameId/401861601/bulgaria-solomon-islands
| team2      = {{fb|BUL}}
| goals1     = 
*[[Raphael Lea'i|Lea'i]] {{goal|29|pen.|70}}
| goals2     = 
*[[Marin Petkov (footballer)|Petkov]] {{goal|5||45+2}}
*[[Tonislav Yordanov|Yordanov]] {{goal|13|pen.}}
*[[Filip Krastev|Krastev]] {{goal|39||44}}
*[[Vladimir Nikolov (footballer)|Nikolov]] {{goal|53||81||90+3}}
*[[Georgi Rusev (footballer)|Rusev]] {{goal|63|pen.}}
*[[Kristiyan Balov|Balov]] {{goal|65}}
| stadium    = [[Gelora Bung Karno Stadium]]
| location   = [[Jakarta]], Indonesia
| attendance =  2,248
| referee    = [[Naufal Adya Fairuski]] ([[Football Association of Indonesia|Indonesia]])
| result     = L
}}
{{footballbox collapsible|format=1
| round      = [[2026 FIFA Series (men's matches)|2026 FIFA Series]]
| date       = 30 March
| time       = {{UTZ|16:45|+7}}
| note       = The kickoff of the Saint Kitts & Nevis v Solomon Islands match, originally scheduled for 15:30 local time, was delayed for 75 minutes due to a [[thunderstorm]] and unfavorable weather conditions.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bolasport.com/read/314366651/cuaca-buruk-dan-sambaran-petir-laga-saint-kitts-and-nevis-vs-kepulauan-solomon-sempat-ditunda-75-menit-di-fifa-series-2026|title=Cuaca Buruk dan Sambaran Petir, Laga Saint Kitts and Nevis vs Kepulauan Solomon Sempat Ditunda 75 Menit di FIFA Series 2026|trans-title=Bad weather and thunderstorms, the kickoff of the Saint Kitts & Nevis vs Solomon Islands match was delayed for 75 minutes in the 2026 FIFA Series|work=BolaSport|date=30 March 2026|language=id-ID}}</ref>)
| team1      = {{fb-rt|SKN}}
| score      = 4–2
| report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/10017/292859/292860/400021849
| team2      = {{fb|SOL}}
| goals1     = 
*[[Omari Sterling-James|Sterling-James]] {{goal|30}}
*[[Philip Mango|Mango]] {{goal|43|o.g.}}
*[[Kyle Kelly|Kelly]] {{goal|75}}
*[[Harry Panayiotou|Panayiotou]] {{goal|87}}
| goals2     = 
*[[Don Keana|Keana]] {{goal|36}}
*[[Junior Fordney|Fordney]] {{goal|85}}
| stadium    = [[Gelora Bung Karno Stadium]]
| location   = [[Jakarta]], Indonesia
| attendance = 
| referee    = [[Thoriq Alkatiri]] ([[Football Association of Indonesia|Indonesia]])
| result     = L
}}

==Coaching staff==
{| class="wikitable"
|-
!style="color:#157D65;background:#FFF200"|Position
!style="color:#157D65;background:#FFF200"|Name
|-
| Head coach || {{flagicon|ENG}} [[Ben Cahn]] (interim)
|align=center| <ref name= "BenCahn">{{cite web
|title=Solomon Islands Football Federation announces Ben Cahn as interim head coach
|url=https://www.siff.com.sb/solomon-islands-football-federation-announces-ben-cahn-as-interim-head-coach/
|date= 21 March 2026 |website= SFF}}</ref>
|-
| Assistant coach || {{flagicon|AUS}} [[Richard Greer]]
|-
| Goalkeeper coach || {{flagicon|SOL}} [[Tibon Oge]]
|-
| Kit Manager || {{flagicon|SOL}} [[Augustine Hou]]
|-
| Team Manager || {{flagicon|SOL}} [[Dixion Lauia Anga]]
|}

===Coaching history===
[[File:Wim Rijsbergen 1978c.jpg|thumb|100px|[[Wim Rijsbergen]] became the manager of the Solomon Islands in 2019]]
:Caretaker managers are listed in ''italics''.
{{div col|colwidth=22em}}
* {{Flagicon|Solomon Islands}} [[Edward Ngara]] (1995–1996)
* {{Flagicon|Solomon Islands}} [[Wilson Maelaua]] (1996)
* {{Flagicon|Scotland}} [[George Cowie]] (2000–2003)
* {{Flagicon|England}} [[Alan Gillett (football)|Alan Gillett]] (2004–2005)
* {{Flagicon|Brazil}} [[Airton Andrioli]] (2006–2009)
* {{Flagicon|Solomon Islands}} [[Jacob Moli]] (2010–2014)<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.fifa.com/associations/association=sol/index.html |title=FIFA.com |date=23 June 2007 |website= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070623100219/http://www.fifa.com/associations/association=sol/index.html |archive-date=23 June 2007 |url-status=dead}}</ref>
* {{Flagicon|Solomon Islands}} [[Moses Toata]] (2015–2016)<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.oceaniafootball.com/ofc/News/ViewArticle/tabid/125/Article/8e7e4eb8-f2a7-40b2-85a4-112359581163/language/en-US/Default.aspx|title=Solomons search for new coach|publisher=[[Oceania Football Confederation]]|date=29 July 2016|access-date=29 July 2016|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160729044059/http://www.oceaniafootball.com/ofc/News/ViewArticle/tabid/125/Article/8e7e4eb8-f2a7-40b2-85a4-112359581163/language/en-US/Default.aspx|archive-date=29 July 2016|df=dmy-all}}</ref>
* {{Flagicon|Spain}} [[Felipe Vega-Arango]] (2017–2018)
* {{Flagicon|Solomon Islands}} [[Moses Toata]] (2018–2019)
* {{Flagicon|Netherlands}} [[Wim Rijsbergen]] (2019)
* {{Flagicon|Solomon Islands}} ''[[Stanley Waita]]'' (2020–2021)
* {{Flagicon|Spain}} [[Felipe Vega-Arango]] (2021–2024)
* {{Flagicon|Solomon Islands}} ''[[Eddie Marahare]]'' (2024)<ref name=":0">{{cite web |title=MOLI APPOINTED FOR NATIONS CUP 2024|url=https://www.siff.com.sb/moli-appointed-for-nations-cup-2024/ |publisher=[[Solomon Islands Football Federation|SIFF]] |access-date=28 June 2024}}</ref>
* {{Flagicon|Solomon Islands}} ''[[Jacob Moli]]'' (2024)<ref name=":0" />
* {{Flagicon|Solomon Islands}} ''[[Moses Toata]]'' (2024)<ref name="JoshSmith">{{Cite web|url=https://www.siff.com.sb/new-head-coach-sets-ambitious-path-ahead/|title=New Head Coach Sets Ambitious Path Ahead
|date= 1 September 2024 |website= SFF}}</ref>
* {{Flagicon|Australia}} Josh Smith (2024–2026)<ref name="JoshSmith" />
* {{flagicon|England}} [[Ben Cahn]] (interim) (2026-)<ref name="BenCahn" /> 
{{div col end}}

==Players==
===Current squad===
The following players were called up for the [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (OFC)|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]] against [[Fiji national football team|Fiji]] on 10 October 2024.<ref>{{cite web|title=Squad Announcement|url=https://www.facebook.com/solomonislandsfootball/posts/pfbid02BrHYfgZA5ExFACvZs9Sv8mueHaZsXKwQkt7K66Xg91dRcbRYSpYrZ9piyFptqcKbl|website=Facebook|publisher=Solomon Islands Football Federation|date=3 October 2024}}</ref>

''Caps and goals correct as of 10 October 2024, after the match against [[Fiji national football team|Fiji]].''

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Philip Mango]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1995|8|28}}|caps=45|goals=0|club=[[Solomon Kings FC|Solomon Kings]]|clubnat=SOL}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Michael Laulae]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2002|5|20}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[Henderson Eels F.C.|Henderson Eels]]|clubnat=SOL}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Harold Nauania]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1997|10|10}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Waneagu United F.C.|Waneagu United]]|clubnat=SOL}}
{{nat fs g break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Javin Wae]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2002|11|17}}|caps=20|goals=0|club=[[Solomon Kings FC|Solomon Kings]]|clubnat=SOL}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Leon Kofana]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2002|6|22}}|caps=21|goals=0|club=[[PNG Hekari FC|PNG Hekari]]|clubnat=PNG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[David Supa]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2000|12|21}}|caps=14|goals=0|club=[[Solomon Kings FC|Solomon Kings]]|clubnat=SOL}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Calvin Ohasio]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2000|4|5}}|caps=12|goals=0|club=[[Central Coast F.C.|Central Coast]]|clubnat=SOL}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Junior David]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2001|9|22}}|caps=10|goals=0|club=[[Solomon Kings FC|Solomon Kings]]|clubnat=SOL}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Alick Stanton]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1998|5|25}}|caps=9|goals=0|club=[[Central Coast F.C.|Central Coast]]|clubnat=SOL}}
{{nat fs g break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Atkin Kaua]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1996|4|4}}|caps=40|goals=5|club=[[Solomon Kings FC|Solomon Kings]]|clubnat=SOL}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[William Komasi]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2000|6|10}}|caps=21|goals=1|club=[[Solomon Kings FC|Solomon Kings]]|clubnat=SOL}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Don Keana]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2000|9|9}}|caps=6|goals=0|club=[[Solomon Kings FC|Solomon  Kings]]|clubnat=SOL}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Carlos Liomasia]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1994|9|17}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Tailevu Naitasiri F.C.|Tailevu]]|clubnat=FIJ}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Ben Fox (Solomon Islands footballer)|Ben Fox]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2001|7|6}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Solomon Kings FC|Solomon Kings]]|clubnat=SOL}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Jared Rongosulia]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1995|11|6}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Navua F.C.|Navua]]|clubnat=FIJ}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Paul Francis (Solomon Islands footballer)|Paul Francis]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2004|9|5}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Real Kakamora F.C.|Real Kakamora]]|clubnat=SOL}}
{{nat fs g break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Micah Lea'alafa]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1991|6|1}}|caps=28|goals=8|club=[[Vipers FC]]|clubnat=AUS}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Alvin Hou]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1996|9|18}}|caps=25|goals=7|club=[[Solomon Warriors F.C.|Solomon Warriors]]|clubnat=SOL}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Raphael Lea'i]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2003|9|9}}|caps=22|goals=11|club=[[Solomon Kings FC|Solomon Kings]]|clubnat=SOL}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Bobby Leslie]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2000|3|3}}|caps=12|goals=2|club=[[Solomon Kings FC|Solomon Kings]]|clubnat=SOL}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Ali Mekawir|Mohammad Mekawir]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2000|7|27}}|caps=7|goals=0|club=[[Navua F.C.|Navua]]|clubnat=FIJ}}
{{nat fs end}}

===Recent call-ups===
The following players have also been called up within the last twelve months.

{{nat fs r start}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Junior Petua]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2003|12|30}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Solomon Kings FC|Solomon Kings]]|clubnat=SOL|latest=v. {{fb|FIJ}}, 20 March 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Timothy Mae'arasia]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1995|6|19}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Solomon Warriors F.C.|Solomon Warriors]]|clubnat=SOL|latest=[[Football at the 2023 Pacific Games – Men's tournament|2023 Pacific Games]]}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Prince Tahanipue]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1995|1|13}}|caps=10|goals=0|club=[[Central Coast F.C.|Central Coast]]|clubnat=SOL|latest=v. {{fb|HKG}}, 5 September 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Loea Taisara]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1989|6|2}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Solomon Warriors F.C.|Solomon Warriors]]|clubnat=SOL|latest=v. {{fb|HKG}}, 5 September 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Allen Peter]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1995|9|11}}|caps=18|goals=0|club=[[Solomon Warriors F.C.|Solomon Warriors]]|clubnat=SOL|latest=[[2024 OFC Men's Nations Cup]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Steven Koti]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2000|6|10}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Kossa F.C.|Kossa]]|clubnat=SOL|latest=[[2024 OFC Men's Nations Cup]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Hadisi Aengari]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1988|10|23}}|caps=44|goals=0|club=[[Solomon Warriors F.C.|Solomon Warriors]]|clubnat=SOL|latest=v. {{fb|FIJ}}, 20 March 2024}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Tigi Molea]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1992|9|24}}|caps=11|goals=3|club=[[Solomon Warriors F.C.|Solomon Warriors]]|clubnat=SOL|latest=v. {{fb|HKG}}, 5 September 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Marlon Tahioa]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1998|11|28}}|caps=9|goals=0|club=[[Central Coast F.C.|Central Coast]]|clubnat=SOL|latest=v. {{fb|HKG}}, 5 September 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Hudson Oreinima]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1988|7|24}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Solomon Kings FC|Solomon Kings]]|clubnat=SOL|latest=[[2024 OFC Men's Nations Cup]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Hadyn Irodao]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2002|10|29}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Central Coast F.C.|Central Coast]]|clubnat=SOL|latest=[[2024 OFC Men's Nations Cup]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Molis Gagame]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1989|9|21}}|caps=14|goals=0|club=[[Kossa F.C.|Kossa]]|clubnat=SOL|latest=v. {{fb|FIJ}}, 20 March 2024}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Joses Nawo]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1988|5|3}}|caps=55|goals=8|club=[[Real Kakamora F.C.|Real Kakamora]]|clubnat=SOL|latest=v. {{fb|HKG}}, 5 September 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Dalton Saeni]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2002|10|14}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Marist F.C.|Marist]]|clubnat=SOL|latest=v. {{fb|HKG}}, 5 September 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Gagame Feni]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1992|8|21}}|caps=35|goals=19|club=[[Solomon Kings FC|Solomon Kings]]|clubnat=SOL|latest=[[2024 OFC Men's Nations Cup]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Junior Fordney]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1999|11|26}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Solomon Kings FC|Solomon Kings]]|clubnat=SOL|latest=[[2024 OFC Men's Nations Cup]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Norman Ngafu]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1997|7|16}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Marist F.C.|Marist]]|clubnat=SOL|latest=[[Football at the 2023 Pacific Games – Men's tournament|2023 Pacific Games]]<sup>PRE</sup>}}
{{nat fs end}}

==Player records==
{{Updated|27 March 2026}}<ref>{{cite web |title=Solomon Islands |url=https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/170/Solomon_Islands.html |website=National Football Teams |access-date=24 April 2021 |archive-date=2 April 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230402153408/https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/170/2023/Solomon_Islands.html |url-status=live }}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Solomon Islands.''

===Most appearances===
[[File:Henry Fa'arodo.jpg|200px|right|thumb|Henry Fa'arodo is the Solomon Islands' most-capped player with 64 appearances.]]

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
!class="unsortable" style="width:175px;"|Name
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Goals
!class="unsortable" style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
|1
|align=left|[[Henry Fa'arodo]]
|64
|20
|2002–2017
|-
|2
|align=left|'''[[Joses Nawo]]'''{{efn|group=players|name=MSG_1|Also appeared in one match against an international 'B' side in the [[MSG Prime Minister's Cup]], however this is not recognised as an official match by FIFA.}}
|55
|8
|2011–present
|-
|3
|align=left|[[Benjamin Totori]]
|52
|29
|2007–2019
|-
|4
|align=left|'''[[Atkin Kaua]]'''{{efn|group=players|name=MSG_2|Also appeared in two matches against international 'B' sides in the [[MSG Prime Minister's Cup]], however these are not recognised as official matches by FIFA.}}
|49
|6
|2016–present
|-
|rowspan=2|5
|align=left|'''[[Hadisi Aengari]]'''
|44
|0
|2011–present
|-
|align=left|'''[[Philip Mango]]'''{{efn|name=MSG_1}}
|45
|0
|2016–present
|-
|rowspan=2|7
|align=left|[[Nelson Sale Kilifa]]
|37
|0
|2004–2017
|-
|align=left|[[Batram Suri]]
|37
|15
|1992–2005
|-
|9
|align=left|[[Commins Menapi]]
|36
|34
|2000–2007
|-
|10
|align="left"|'''[[Gagame Feni]]'''
|35
|19
|2012–present
|-
|}

===Top goalscorers===

{|class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
!class="unsortable" width=175px|Name
!width=50px|Goals
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Ratio
!class="unsortable" width=100px|Career
|-
|1
|align="left"|[[Commins Menapi]]
|34
|36
|{{#expr:34/36 round 2}}
|2000–2007
|-
|2
|align="left"|[[Benjamin Totori]]
|29
|52
|{{#expr:29/52 round 2}}
|2007–2019
|-
|3
|align="left"|[[Henry Fa'arodo]]
|20
|64
|{{#expr:20/64 round 2}}
|2002–2017
|-
|4
|align="left"|'''[[Gagame Feni]]'''
|19
|35
|{{#expr:19/35 round 2}}
|2012–present
|-
|5
|align="left"|[[Batram Suri]]
|15
|37
|{{#expr:15/37 round 2}}
|1992–2005
|-
|6
|align="left"|'''[[Raphael Lea'i]]'''{{efn|group=players|name=Lea'i|Lea'i also appeared in two matches, scoring one goal, against international 'B' sides in the [[MSG Prime Minister's Cup]], however these are not recognised as official matches by FIFA.}}
|14
|32
|{{#expr:14/32 round 2}}
|2022–present
|-
|7
|align="left"|[[Noel Berry]]
|10
|15
|{{#expr:10/15 round 2}}
|1995–2000
|-
|8
|align="left"|'''[[Micah Lea'alafa]]'''{{efn|name=MSG_1}}
|9
|31
|{{#expr:9/31 round 2}}
|2016–present
|-
|rowspan=2|9
|align="left"|'''[[John Orobulu]]'''{{efn|name=MSG_1}}
|8
|16
|{{#expr:8/16 round 2}}
|2018–present
|-
|align="left"|'''[[Joses Nawo]]'''{{efn|name=MSG_1}}
|8
|55
|{{#expr:8/55 round 2}}
|2011–present
|-
|}
{{notelist|group=players}}

==Competitive record==

===FIFA World Cup===
{|class="wikitable nowrap" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[FIFA World Cup]] record
!rowspan=39|
!colspan=7|[[FIFA World Cup qualification|Qualification]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pos.|Position in qualification group stage or in all qualification}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|-
|{{flagicon|Uruguay}} [[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=13|''Not member of FIFA''
|colspan=7 rowspan=13|''Not member of FIFA''
|-
|{{flagicon|ITA|1861}} [[1934 FIFA World Cup|1934]]
|-
|{{flagicon|France|1794}} [[1938 FIFA World Cup|1938]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Brazil|1889}} [[1950 FIFA World Cup|1950]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Switzerland}} [[1954 FIFA World Cup|1954]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Sweden}} [[1958 FIFA World Cup|1958]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Chile}} [[1962 FIFA World Cup|1962]]
|-
|{{flagicon|ENG}} [[1966 FIFA World Cup|1966]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Mexico}} [[1970 FIFA World Cup|1970]]
|-
|{{flagicon|West Germany}} [[1974 FIFA World Cup|1974]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Argentina}} [[1978 FIFA World Cup|1978]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Spain}} [[1982 FIFA World Cup|1982]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Mexico}} [[1986 FIFA World Cup|1986]]
|-
|{{flagicon|ITA}} [[1990 FIFA World Cup|1990]]
|colspan="8"|''Did not enter''
|colspan="7"|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[1994 FIFA World Cup|1994]]|| colspan="8" rowspan="9" |''Did not qualify''
|[[1994 FIFA World Cup qualification (OFC)|1st Round]]||4||0||1||3||5||13
|-
|{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]]
|[[1998 FIFA World Cup qualification (OFC)|2nd Round]]||8||3||3||2||22||23
|-
|{{flagicon|South Korea|1997}} {{flagicon|Japan}} [[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]]
|[[2002 FIFA World Cup qualification (OFC)|1st Round]]||4||2||0||2||17||10
|-
|{{flagicon|Germany}} [[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]]
|[[2006 FIFA World Cup qualification (OFC)|2nd]]||11||6||2||3||24||16
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010]]
|[[2010 FIFA World Cup qualification (OFC)|SF]]||6||4||0||2||23||6
|-
|{{flagicon|Brazil}} [[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]]
|[[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification (OFC)|4th]]||9||2||2||5||7||22
|-
|{{flagicon|Russia}} [[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018]]
|[[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification (OFC)|2nd]]||9||4||1||4||10||16
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2022 FIFA World Cup|2022]]
|[[2022 FIFA World Cup qualification (OFC)|2nd]]||3||2||0||1||6||8
|-
|{{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States of America}} [[2026 FIFA World Cup|2026]]
|[[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (OFC)|2nd Round]]||3||1||0||2||4||5
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} {{flagicon|Portugal}} {{flagicon|Spain}} [[2030 FIFA World Cup|2030]]
| colspan="8" rowspan="2" |''To be determined''
| colspan="7" rowspan="2" |''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}} [[2034 FIFA World Cup|2034]]
|-
!Total
!{{sortdash}}
!0/23
!–
!–
!–
!–
!–
!–
!—
!60
!24
!9
!27
!122
!126
|}

===OFC Nations Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;"
!colspan="10"|[[OFC Men's Nations Cup|Oceania Cup / OFC Nations Cup]] record
!rowspan="26"|
!colspan=6|Qualification record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Matches played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!Squad
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Matches played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|New Zealand}} [[1973 OFC Nations Cup|1973]]||colspan="9"|''Did not enter''||colspan="6"|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|New Caledonia|1853}} [[1980 OFC Nations Cup|1980]]||Group stage||8th||3||0||0||3||3||21||{{n/a}}||colspan=6|''No qualification''
|- bgcolor="#cc9966"
|{{flagicon|Pacific Community}} [[1996 OFC Nations Cup|1996]]||Semi-finals||3rd||2||0||0||2||1||3||[[1996 OFC Nations Cup squads#Solomon Islands|Squad]]||4||4||0||0||10||1
|-
|{{flagicon|Australia}} [[1998 OFC Nations Cup|1998]]||colspan="9"|''Did not qualify''||4||2||1||1||8||7
|-bgcolor="#cc9966"
|{{flagicon|Tahiti}} [[2000 OFC Nations Cup|2000]]||Third place||3rd||4||2||0||2||7||10||[[2000 OFC Nations Cup squads#Solomon Islands|Squad]]||4||2||1||1||10||9
|-
|{{flagicon|New Zealand}} [[2002 OFC Nations Cup|2002]]||Group stage||6th||3||0||1||2||3||9||[[2002 OFC Nations Cup squads#Solomon Islands|Squad]]||colspan=6|''Qualified automatically''
|-bgcolor="silver"
|{{flagicon|Australia}} [[2004 OFC Nations Cup|2004]]||[[2004 OFC Nations Cup Final|Runners-up]]||2nd||7||3||1||3||10||17||[[2004 OFC Nations Cup squads#Solomon Islands|Squad]]||4||3||1||0||14||1
|-
||{{flagicon|Pacific Community}} [[2008 OFC Nations Cup|2008]]||colspan="9"|''Did not qualify''||6||4||0||2||23||6
|-
|style="border: 3px solid red" bgcolor="LemonChiffon"|{{flagicon|Solomon Islands}} [[2012 OFC Nations Cup|2012]]
|bgcolor="LemonChiffon"|Fourth place
|bgcolor="LemonChiffon"|4th
|bgcolor="LemonChiffon"|5
|bgcolor="LemonChiffon"|1
|bgcolor="LemonChiffon"|2
|bgcolor="LemonChiffon"|2
|bgcolor="LemonChiffon"|5
|bgcolor="LemonChiffon"|6
|bgcolor="LemonChiffon"|[[2012 OFC Nations Cup squads#Solomon Islands|Squad]]||colspan=6 rowspan=3|''Qualified automatically''
|- bgcolor="#cc9966"
|{{flagicon|Papua New Guinea}} [[2016 OFC Nations Cup|2016]]
|Semi-finals
|3rd
|4
|1
|0
|3
|2
|4
|[[2016 OFC Nations Cup squads#Solomon Islands|Squad]]
|-
|{{flagicon|FIJ}} {{flagicon|VAN}} [[2024 OFC Men's Nations Cup|2024]]||Group stage
|6th
|2
|0
|0
|2
|0
|4
|[[2024 OFC Men's Nations Cup squads#Solomon Islands|Squad]]
|-
!Total
!Runners-up
!8/11
!30
!7
!4
!19
!31
!74
!—
!22
!15
!3
!4
!65
!24
|}

===Pacific Games===
{|class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;"
!colspan="9"|[[Pacific Games]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|- style="background:LemonChiffon;"
|{{flagicon|FIJ|colonial}} [[Football at the 1963 South Pacific Games|1963]]
|Fourth place
|4th
|3
|1
|0
|2
|6
|26
|-
|{{flagicon|NCL|french}} [[Football at the 1966 South Pacific Games|1966]]
|Group stage
|6th
|2
|0
|1
|1
|4
|12
|-
|{{flagicon|PNG|1965}} [[Football at the 1969 South Pacific Games|1969]]
|Sixth place
|6th
|5
|0
|1
|4
|8
|19
|-
|{{flagicon|TAH|1946}} [[Football at the 1971 South Pacific Games|1971]]
|Colspan=8|''did not enter''
|- style="background:#CC9966;"
|{{flagicon|Guam}} [[Football at the 1975 South Pacific Games|1975]]
|Bronze medal 
|3rd
|4
|2
|1
|1
|9
|8
|- style="background:#CC9966;"
|{{flagicon|FIJ}} [[Football at the 1979 South Pacific Games|1979]]
|Bronze medal 
|3rd
|5
|4
|0
|1
|24
|5
|-
|{{flagicon|SAM}} [[Football at the 1983 South Pacific Games|1983]]
|Group stage
|10th
|3
|1
|0
|2
|0
|11
|-
|{{flagicon|NCL|french}} [[Football at the 1987 South Pacific Games|1987]]
|Colspan=8|''did not enter''
|- style="background:silver"
|{{flagicon|PNG}} [[Football at the 1991 South Pacific Games|1991]]
|Silver medal 
|2nd
|5
|4
|1
|0
|12
|3
|- style="background:silver"
|{{flagicon|TAH}} [[Football at the 1995 South Pacific Games|1995]]
|Silver medal 
|2nd
|6
|4
|0
|2
|34
|10
|-
|{{flagicon|FIJ}} [[Football at the 2003 South Pacific Games – Men's tournament|2003]]
|Group stage
|5th
|4
|2
|1
|1
|14
|4
|- style="background:LemonChiffon;"
|{{flagicon|SAM}} [[Football at the 2007 South Pacific Games – Men's tournament|2007]]
|Fourth place
|4th
|6
|4
|0
|2
|23
|6
|- style="background:silver"
|{{flagicon|NCL|football}} [[Football at the 2011 Pacific Games – Men's tournament|2011]]
|Silver medal 
|2nd
|7
|5
|0
|2
|21
|6
|-
|{{flagicon|PNG}} [[Football at the 2015 Pacific Games – Men's tournament|2015]]
|colspan=8|''See [[Solomon Islands national under-23 football team]]''
|-
|{{flagicon|SAM}} [[Football at the 2019 Pacific Games – Men's tournament|2019]]
|Group stage
|7th
|5
|2
|1
|2
|30
|9
|-
|- style="background:silver"
|{{flagicon|SOL}} [[Football at the 2023 Pacific Games – Men's tournament|2023]]
|Silver medal
|2nd
|5
|2
|1
|2
|30
|9
|-
!Total
!Runners-up
!12/15
!55
!29
!6
!20
!185
!119
|}

===Wantok Cup===
* [[July 2008 first Wantok Cup|2008 (July, 1st edition)]]&nbsp;– '''First place'''

==Head-to-head record==
Updated 27 March 2026 after the match against [[Bulgaria national football team|Bulgaria]]
{{#invoke:sports table|main|style=WDL
|show_positions=n
|ranking_style=wperc
|show_totals=yes
|sortable_table=yes

|team1=ASA|name_ASA={{fb|ASA}}
|team2=AUS|name_AUS={{fb|AUS}}
|team3=BUL|name_BUL={{fb|BUL}}
|team4=TPE|name_TPE={{fb|TPE}}
|team5=COK|name_COK={{fb|COK}}
|team6=FIJ|name_FIJ={{fb|FIJ}}
|team7=GUM|name_GUM={{fb|GUM}}
|team8=HKG|name_HKG={{fb|HKG}}
|team9=KIR|name_KIR={{fb|KIR}}
|team10=MAC|name_MAC={{fb|MAC}}
|team11=MAS|name_MAS={{fb|MAS}}
|team12=NCL|name_NCL={{fb|NCL}}
|team13=NZL|name_NZL={{fb|NZL}}
|team14=PNG|name_PNG={{fb|PNG}}
|team15=SAM|name_SAM={{fb|SAM}}|note_SAM=Includes results as Western Samoa.
|team16=SIN|name_SIN={{fb|SIN}}
|team17=TAH|name_TAH={{fb|TAH}}
|team18=TGA|name_TGA={{fb|TGA}}
|team19=TUV|name_TUV={{fb|TUV}}
|team20=VAN|name_VAN={{fb|VAN}}|note_VAN=Includes results as New Hebrides.
|team21=WAF|name_WAF={{fb|WAF|local}}

|win_ASA=4 |draw_ASA=0 |loss_ASA=0 |gf_ASA=40|ga_ASA=1
|win_AUS=0 |draw_AUS=1 |loss_AUS=9 |gf_AUS=8 |ga_AUS=55
|win_BUL=0 |draw_BUL=0 |loss_BUL=1 |gf_BUL=2 |ga_BUL=10
|win_TPE=1 |draw_TPE=0 |loss_TPE=0 |gf_TPE=1 |ga_TPE=0
|win_COK=5 |draw_COK=0 |loss_COK=0 |gf_COK=37|ga_COK=2
|win_FIJ=8 |draw_FIJ=16|loss_FIJ=20|gf_FIJ=47|ga_FIJ=79
|win_GUM=3 |draw_GUM=0 |loss_GUM=0 |gf_GUM=24|ga_GUM=2
|win_HKG=0 |draw_HKG=0 |loss_HKG=1 |gf_HKG=0 |ga_HKG=3
|win_KIR=1 |draw_KIR=0 |loss_KIR=0 |gf_KIR=7 |ga_KIR=0
|win_MAC=1 |draw_MAC=0 |loss_MAC=0 |gf_MAC=4 |ga_MAC=1
|win_MAS=0 |draw_MAS=0 |loss_MAS=1 |gf_MAS=1 |ga_MAS=4
|win_NCL=11|draw_NCL=3 |loss_NCL=15|gf_NCL=35|ga_NCL=58
|win_NZL=0 |draw_NZL=2 |loss_NZL=12|gf_NZL=11|ga_NZL=52
|win_PNG=15|draw_PNG=4 |loss_PNG=7 |gf_PNG=44|ga_PNG=34
|win_SAM=4 |draw_SAM=0 |loss_SAM=0 |gf_SAM=21|ga_SAM=0
|win_SIN=0 |draw_SIN=1 |loss_SIN=1 |gf_SIN=4 |ga_SIN=5
|win_TAH=5 |draw_TAH=3 |loss_TAH=16|gf_TAH=26|ga_TAH=76
|win_TGA=5 |draw_TGA=0 |loss_TGA=0 |gf_TGA=31|ga_TGA=0
|win_TUV=4 |draw_TUV=0 |loss_TUV=0 |gf_TUV=29|ga_TUV=1
|win_VAN=23|draw_VAN=7 |loss_VAN=7 |gf_VAN=82|ga_VAN=40
|win_WAF=3 |draw_WAF=0 |loss_WAF=0 |gf_WAF=23|ga_WAF=1

|update=complete|source=[[Solomon Islands national football team results (1963–1999)#Results|1963–99 results]] and [[Solomon Islands national football team results (2000–present)#Results|2000–present results]]
}}

== Honours ==
===Continental===
* '''[[OFC Nations Cup]]'''
** {{Silver2}} Runners-up (1): [[2004 OFC Nations Cup|2004]]
** {{Bronze3}} Third place (1): [[2000 OFC Nations Cup|2000]]

===Regional===
* '''[[Football at the Pacific Games|Pacific Games]]'''
** {{Silver2}} Silver medal (4): [[1991 South Pacific Games|1991]], [[1995 South Pacific Games|1995]], [[2011 Pacific Games|2011]], [[Football at the 2023 Pacific Games – Men's tournament|2023]]
** {{Bronze3}} Bronze medal (2): [[1975 South Pacific Games|1975]], [[1979 South Pacific Games|1979]]
* '''[[MSG Prime Minister's Cup|Melanesia Cup / MSG Prime Minister's Cup]]'''
** {{Gold1}} '''Champions (2)''': [[1994 Melanesia Cup|1994]], [[2023 MSG Prime Minister's Cup|2023]]
** {{Silver2}} Runners-up (2): [[1988 Melanesia Cup|1988]], [[2000 Melanesia Cup|2000]]
** {{Bronze3}} Third place (4): [[1989 Melanesia Cup|1989]], [[1992 Melanesia Cup|1992]], [[1998 Melanesia Cup|1998]], [[2024 MSG Prime Minister's Cup|2024]]

===Awards===
* '''[[OFC Nations Cup awards#Fair Play Award|OFC Nations Cup Fair Play Award]]''': 2012

===Summary===
{| class="wikitable" width=30% style="font-size:90%; text-align:center;"
|-
!Competition !!{{gold1}} !!{{silver2}} !!{{bronze3}} !!Total
|-
|align=left|[[OFC Men's Nations Cup|OFC Nations Cup]]
|0||1||1||2
|-
!Total!!0!!1!!1!!2
|}

==Historical kits==

{{Empty section|date=August 2024}}

==References==
{{reflist}}

==Further reading==
*{{cite web|url=https://khelnow.com/football/2023-06-indian-football-hero-intercontinental-cup-rival-watch-vanuatu|title=Hero Intercontinental Cup Rival Watch: Vanuatu|website=khelnow.com|publisher=Khel Now India|date=4 June 2023|first=Uttiyo|last=Sarkar|access-date=15 June 2023|archive-date=4 June 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230604190649/https://khelnow.com/football/2023-06-indian-football-hero-intercontinental-cup-rival-watch-vanuatu}}

==External links==
* [http://www.siff.com.sb/ Solomon Islands Football Federation official website]
* [https://www.oceaniafootball.com/solomon-islands/ Solomon Islands] at [[Oceania Football Confederation|OFC]]
* [https://www.fifa.com/about-fifa/associations/SOL Solomon Islands] at [[FIFA]]

{{Navboxes
| title = Solomon Islands squads
| bg        = #215B33
| fg        = #FCD116
| bordercolor = #0051BA
| list =
{{Solomon Islands squad 2000 OFC Nations Cup}}
{{Solomon Islands squad 2002 OFC Nations Cup}}
{{Solomon Islands squad 2004 OFC Nations Cup}}
{{Solomon Islands squad 2012 OFC Nations Cup}}
{{Solomon Islands squad 2016 OFC Nations Cup}}
{{Solomon Islands squad 2024 OFC Men's Nations Cup}}
}}
{{Football in the Solomon Islands}}
{{OFC teams}}
{{National sports teams of the Solomon Islands}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:Solomon Islands Men's National Football Team}}
[[Category:Solomon Islands national football team| ]]
[[Category:Solomon Islands men's national football teams| ]]
[[Category:Oceanian national association football teams]]
